name: Masticatory Muscle Myositis (MMM)
description: MMM is an immune-mediated inflammatory disease targeting the masticatory muscles (temporalis and masseter), which have a unique muscle fibre type (2M) not found elsewhere in the body. It causes pain, trismus (jaw locking), and muscle atrophy.
symptoms: Jaw pain and reluctance to eat
treatment_overview: High-dose immunosuppressive prednisolone is highly effective if started early. Long-term tapering doses are needed to prevent relapse. Early treatment before muscle fibrosis develops gives the best outcomes.
prevention_tips: Prompt investigation of jaw pain or swelling
